methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deepnight is a Seed-stage defense & security company that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables spanning Seed and Series A: Tenna Systems ($60M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $5M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $12M. Peer-median analysis across the four comparables produces a $225M implied valuation, with a range of $60M to $500M. The stage-progression method reflects Deepnight's early maturity, while the peer-median approach captures the market ceiling observed in the Defense & Security sector for similar-stage companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $119M across both methods. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate comparable selection: Tenna Systems, a Seed peer, implies tighter guidance near $11M–$60M, while Series A peers suggest substantially higher values. Key uncertainties include Deepnight's product maturity relative to named comparables, capital efficiency trajectory, and whether the company's technology positioning maps to Seed or early Series A stage multiples within the defense sector.
